{{Infobox concept
| name = Soulbound
| introduced = Of Sea and Shadow
}}

''See also: [[Soulbound Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Soulbound are individuals who have formed a deep, mystical bond with an [[Awakened object]] known as a [[Vessel]]. This connection is achieved through processes involving [[Intent]], [[Reading]], and sometimes [[alchemy]] or the integration of [[Elder]] components. Once bonded, a Soulbound can freely wield the supernatural abilities of an [[Elder]] or a [[Kameira]], granting them powers that range from physical enhancements and elemental manipulation to the ability to summon fire or create thorny vines. The specific nature of a Soulbound's abilities is determined by the characteristics and [[Intent]] of their [[Vessel]]. While some individuals use [[Vessels]] as tools for craftsmanship, others serve as elite warriors or vital political figures within the [[Empire]]. The relationship between a Soulbound and their [[Vessel]] is profound; the object becomes an intrinsic part of the individual, often sharing its desires or even possessing a form of consciousness that can be heard as a voice. This bond can be intense and difficult to manage, particularly during the initial months of bonding when stability is low and the [[Vessel]]'s urges can be overwhelming. In combat, a Soulbound's power is tied directly to their [[Vessel]]; if the object is identified and seized or destroyed, the individual's ability to fight effectively is compromised. Beyond human users, certain ships are also considered Soulbound when they are [[Awakened]] and bound to their captains through the use of [[Elder]] parts or artifacts.}}

== Of Sea and Shadow ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Soulbound are a rare type of human, numbering only a handful on the continent, who gain useful powers by borrowing from [[Kameira]] or the [[Elders]]. This status is achieved through a process called [[Awakening]], and while it can take years to bond a [[Vessel]], every Soulbound maintains a connection to their [[Vessel]]. Although the [[Greenwardens]] are believed to all be Soulbound, these individuals are actually the greatest warriors of the [[Empire]]. Within the crew, two known Soulbound exist, including [[Naberius Clayborn]], and it is noted that a Soulbound who has breached [[Bastion]]’s [[Veil]] can wield power freely. Despite their strength, a Soulbound would be unable to stand against the focused power of [[Urzaia]]'s opponent, though they can fight their way to freedom if brought to their [[Vessel]].}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Greenwardens]] are supposedly all [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c4">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Soulbound]] are humans who borrow the powers of [[Elders]] or [[Kameira]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c5">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|There were only a handful of [[Soulbound]] on the continent.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Soulbound]] individuals possess powers that can be useful.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c6">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|It can take years for a [[Soulbound]] to bond a [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c14">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Soulbound]] are created through the process of [[Awakening]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c15">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|There are two known [[Soulbound]] on the crew.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c16">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|A [[Champion]] is more than just a [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c17">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|A [[Soulbound]] has breached [[Bastion]]’s [[Veil]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c20">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|A [[Soulbound]] can wield power freely.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c21">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|Every [[Soulbound]] can feel her [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c21" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Calder]] wonders if [[Jerri]] has become a [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c22">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Soulbound]] is a status or type of person.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|A [[Soulbound]] would not stand a chance against the power of [[Urzaia]]'s opponent if she focused it.<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c23">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Naberius Clayborn]] is a [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c23" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|A [[Soulbound]] can fight their way free if they are brought to their [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c25">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow|[[Soulbound]] are the greatest warriors of the [[Empire]].<ref name="ee-sea-01-of-sea-and-shadow-c26">''Of Sea and Shadow'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Of Shadow and Sea ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|A Soulbound is a person who has bonded with an object of power known as a [[Vessel]], from which they may draw specific powers. These entities are defined by their complex relationship to [[Intent]], and certain types exist, such as the Soulbound [[Silent One]]. While [[Navigators]] are considered Soulbound because they are bound to their ships, others like [[Bastion]] have held this status in the past. The nature of a Soulbound is so significant that [[Gardeners]] avoid fighting them at all costs, and the presence of one in a crater can determine the success of an individual like [[Shera]]. Furthermore, if [[Naberius Clayborn]] were to take the [[Heart]] as his [[Vessel]], he would undergo this transformation.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Fletcher]] compares the envelope to a [[Soulbound]]'s [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c2">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Soulbound]] are entities whose relationship to [[Intent]] is a subject of concern.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c4">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|If [[Naberius Clayborn]] takes the [[Heart]] as his [[Soulbound Vessel]], he would become a [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|A [[Soulbound]] [[Silent One]] is a type of entity.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c6">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Soulbound]] are beings that [[Gardeners]] avoid fighting at all costs.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Soulbound]] draw their power from a [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c8">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Each [[Navigator]] is a [[Soulbound]], bound to their ship.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c10">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Shera]]'s chance of success depends on whether a [[Soulbound]] is in the crater.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Bastion]] was a [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c12">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|A [[Soulbound]] is a person who has bonded with an object of power.<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c19">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Soulbound]] individuals may have powers granted by their [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea-c25">''Of Shadow and Sea'', chapter 25</ref>}}

== Of Dawn and Darkness ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|A Soulbound is a legendary fighter and a type of person [[Jyrine]] has long dreamed of meeting. Born through the phenomena of [[Reading]] and [[Intent]], these individuals possess powers that remain not fully understood and serve as entities that flare like a beacon within [[The Testament]]'s awareness. Because conventional arms are ineffective against them, Soulbound individuals and combat-capable modifications have been mobilized, as they are people who possess something to bind. These entities can call for their [[Vessel]] and were notably involved in the attack on [[Mekendi Maxeus]]. Among them is [[Shera]], whose specific level of danger compared to other Soulbound remains a subject of inquiry for [[Jerri]].}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|A [[Soulbound]] is a type of person [[Jyrine]] has dreamed about meeting.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c1">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The [[Soulbound]] is a type of entity that flares like a beacon in [[The Testament]]'s awareness.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c2">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|A [[Soulbound]] is a person who has something to bind.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c4">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|The birth of a [[Soulbound]] is a phenomenon of [[Reading]] and [[Intent]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c5">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Soulbound]] and combat-capable modifications were mobilized because conventional arms are ineffective.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Soulbound]] individuals possess powers that are not fully understood.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c7">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|A [[Soulbound]] can call for their [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c11">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Shera]] is a [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c12">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Jerri]] asks what makes [[Shera]] more dangerous than any other [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|[[Soulbound]] is a classification or type of person that [[Calder]] asks [[Duster]] about.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c13">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|A [[Soulbound]] was involved in the attack on [[Mekendi Maxeus]].<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c14">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|A [[Soulbound]] is a type of legendary fighter.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c15">''Of Dawn and Darkness'', chapter 15</ref>}}

== Of Darkness and Dawn ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|As one of the world's first Soulbound, [[Alagaeus]] belongs to a vital group within the [[Empire]] that possesses a link to specific [[Vessels]], such as a [[pistol]]. These individuals derive their power from either an [[Elder]] or a [[Kameira]], and the first few months of their existence are critical for harnessing this [[Elder power]]. While a Soulbound can draw strength by inhaling through a blade, they may also exist without their [[Vessel]], as seen with [[Shera]]. Although the [[Imperial Guard]] is capable of subduing them, mortal judges find they cannot enforce their will upon Soulbound with any degree of impartiality or precision. This group includes individuals like [[Jyrine]] and remains essential to the stability of the [[Empire]].}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Alagaeus]] is one of the first [[Soulbound]] in the world.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c1">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Soulbound]] involves a link between an individual and their [[Vessels]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|A [[Soulbound]] is a person who might use a [[pistol]] as a [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|The first few months of a [[Soulbound]] are critical when they harness [[Elder power]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c4">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|A [[Soulbound]] is a person who has power from an [[Elder]] or a [[Kameira]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c8">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|A [[Soulbound]] is a person who possesses a [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c10">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|Mortal judges cannot enforce their will on [[Soulbound]] with precision or impartiality.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c11">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Soulbound]] is a group that the [[Imperial Guard]] is capable of subduing.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c13">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Jyrine]] is a [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c15">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Shera]] is a [[Soulbound]] without her [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c17">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|A [[Soulbound]] can draw power by inhaling through a blade.<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c21">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn|[[Soulbound]] are vital to the [[Empire]].<ref name="ee-shadow-02-of-darkness-and-dawn-c22">''Of Darkness and Dawn'', chapter 22</ref>}}

== Of Kings and Killers ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|Soulbound is a type of magic that can be an achievement, a state of being, or a power possessed by [[Luminian Order]] [[Pilgrims]]. This ability allows individuals to perform immense feats, such as reshaping the [[Spear of Tharlos]], projecting illusions to lure [[Bliss]] away from the [[Capital]], or even raising an [[island]] from the sea. While alchemists utilize leashed Soulbound, those who possess this power must leave their [[Vessels]] in secure storage before meetings, an experience that feels like a part of their body going numb. Although Soulbound individuals can control their [[Vessels]] through contact, the [[Luminian Order]]'s support means very few remain capable of healing injuries. The nature of one's Soulbound is subject to specific requirements for [[Champions]], and the power is so potent that [[Alsa Grayweather]] would stand no chance in a battle between such beings.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|A [[Soulbound]] is someone [[Calder]] cannot defend himself from if [[Jerri]] is unarmed.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c2">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Soulbound]] individuals are requested to leave their [[Vessels]] in secure storage outside the meeting venue.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c4">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Varia Selethir]] is [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c5">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Alsa Grayweather]] would stand no chance in a battle between [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c6">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|For a [[Soulbound]], having their [[Vessel]] locked away is like having a part of their body go numb.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Estyr]] displays open bloodlust of the ancient [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Alchemists]] use leashed [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c7">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Soulbound]] individuals are capable of raising an [[island]] from the sea.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c8">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Soulbound]] is a type of magic.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c13">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|A [[Soulbound]] was hired to project illusions to lure [[Bliss]] away from the [[Capital]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Jerri]] is maintaining a cover as a [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c14">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Soulbound]] individuals can control their [[Vessel]] through contact.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|The [[Luminian Order]]'s support leaves few [[Soulbound]] capable of healing injuries.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c17">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|A [[Soulbound]] is a power that [[Luminian Order]] [[Pilgrims]] can possess.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c17" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Soulbound]] is a state or achievement one might reach.<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c18">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Champions]] have requirements regarding the nature of their [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c19">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers|[[Soulbound]] is a power [[Bliss]] used to reshape the [[Spear of Tharlos]].<ref name="ee-sea-03-of-kings-and-killers-c23">''Of Kings and Killers'', chapter 23</ref>}}

== Of Killers and Kings ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Soulbound are volatile individuals who can be newly acquired through a process that allows people to emerge successfully transformed. These entities possess powers of communication and, in some historical instances, the ability to alter their voice and appearance. While they have unleashed their powers within the streets of the [[Imperial Palace]], a true challenge for them lies in plumbing the depths of their [[Vessel]] and uncovering layers of [[Intent]]. Because powerful Soulbound are capable of destroying the [[Shades of Urg’naut]], [[Shera]] maintains a small army of them and has expressed a need for more combat-capable members. Their strength is such that awakened weapons have even assaulted the [[Dead Mother]] in a manner resembling an army of Soulbound.}}

{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Soulbound]] are volatile individuals.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c4">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Soulbound]] unleashed their powers in the streets of the [[Imperial Palace]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c8">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|The challenge of a [[Soulbound]] is to uncover the layers of [[Intent]] and plumb the depths of their [[Vessel]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c14">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|A [[Soulbound]] once existed with the ability to change appearance and voice.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c14" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|Powerful [[Soulbound]] can destroy the [[Shades of Urg’naut]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c20">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Shera]] states they need more combat-capable [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Soulbound]] have powers of communication.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Soulbound]] are entities that can be newly acquired.<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Soulbinding]] is a process where people can come out successfully [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Shera]] has a small army of [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c25">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings|[[Awakened weapons]] assault the [[Dead Mother]] like an army of [[Soulbound]].<ref name="ee-shadow-03-of-killers-and-kings-c26">''Of Killers and Kings'', chapter 26</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|“Conventional arms seem ineffective, so we mobilized all Soulbound and combat-capable modifications.<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness|“Pardon me, Great One. But what makes her more dangerous than any other Soulbound?”<ref name="ee-sea-02-of-dawn-and-darkness-c12" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Elder Empire nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:Of Sea and Shadow]]
[[Category:Of Dawn and Darkness]]
[[Category:Of Kings and Killers]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
[[Category:Of Darkness and Dawn]]
[[Category:Of Killers and Kings]]
[[Category:Elder Empire]]
[[Category:Elder Empire concepts]]
[[Category:Terminology]]
